summary: Iteration 9 auto-revert on empty cycle deletion fully validated. All 7 backend pytest scenarios pass; legacy entry scenario verified via direct MongoDB insertion. DELETE /api/entries/{id} returns {ok:true, reverted_to_cycle:<id|null>} and correctly reopens previous closed cycle (with restored snapshots) only when the deleted entry was the last in the current OPEN cycle.
tests_executed: test_non_last_entry_delete_no_revert - reverted_to_cycle null, cycle stays open
test_last_entry_with_prev_closed_cycle_triggers_revert - empty new cycle deleted, prior closed cycle reopened, dashboard.cycle.total_driving_minutes restored, days_worked correct
test_last_entry_no_prev_closed_cycle_no_revert - no revert; cycle stays open and empty
test_delete_from_closed_cycle_no_revert - deleting any entry (incl. last) from a CLOSED cycle never triggers revert
test_after_revert_new_entry_attaches_to_reopened_cycle - subsequent POST /api/entries attaches to reopened cycle (not a fresh one)
test_revert_picks_most_recent_closed_cycle - among multiple closed cycles, the most recently closed one is reopened
legacy cycle_id=null deletion (direct mongo insertion) - reverted_to_cycle null, current open cycle untouched
critical_code_review_comments: delete_entry logic uses find_one to read cycle including _id; not serialized (no return of _id) so fine, but consider projecting {'_id': 0} for consistency with other reads.
server.py is ~700 lines; near the recommended split threshold. Future iterations should consider splitting into auth/entries/cycles/summary modules.
Previous-cycle lookup sort=[('ended_at', -1)] relies on ISO-string ordering which is correct for UTC isoformat strings but worth documenting.
Auto-revert path correctly recomputes cycle counters on the reopened cycle so dashboard.total_driving_minutes is restored.
test_credentials: admin@routier-facile.fr / Admin123! (loaded from /app/memory/test_credentials.md); fresh TEST_revert_* users registered per test
seed_data_creation: Each test registers a fresh TEST_revert_<uuid>@example.com user; legacy mongo-insert test cleans up entries/cycles/user after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-revert delete behavior is locked in by /app/backend/tests/test_auto_revert_cycle.py. Frontend EditEntry toast wording ('Journée supprimée — retour au cycle précédent' vs 'Journée supprimée') was deprioritized in this iteration; if tested next, mock or trigger both paths by setting up cycles via API before navigating to EditEntry.
